primary_purpose: Declares the public C API, type aliases, data structures, and function list used by TIC-80 cartridges and core modules. Provides the bridge between scripting VMs and engine internals.

key_structures.name: RemapResult
key_structures.description: Small POD that holds sprite index, flip and rotate info returned by Remap callback when drawing maps.

key_structures.name: tic_tick_data
key_structures.description: Holds function pointers for trace, error, exit callbacks and timing counters passed to each game tick.

key_structures.name: tic_point
key_structures.description: Simple 2-D integer coordinate pair used for mouse and generic geometry.

key_structures.name: tic_rect
key_structures.description: Rectangle expressed as x, y, width, height.

key_structures.name: tic_blit_callback
key_structures.description: Bundle of optional per-scanline and border callbacks used by renderer.

key_structures.name: tic_mem
key_structures.description: Top-level memory context exposed to game code, containing product header, RAM pointer, cartridge data, runtime flags and input state bits.

public_api.name: TIC_API_LIST
public_api.signature: macro expanding to ~60 functions like tic_api_print, tic_api_cls, tic_api_pix, etc.
public_api.purpose: Defines all draw, input, memory and audio functions callable by scripts.

public_api.name: tic_core_create
public_api.purpose: Factory to allocate and initialise a new console core instance.

public_api.name: tic_core_close
public_api.purpose: Destroy previously created core and release memory.

public_api.name: tic_core_tick
public_api.purpose: Process one frame of emulation/game logic, invoking script callbacks and rendering.

public_api.name: tic_core_blit
public_api.purpose: Render prepared frame buffer to host video layer.

memory_regions.name: tic_mem->ram
memory_regions.purpose: Runtime RAM consisting of VRAM, palette, map, sound and user scratch space.

memory_regions.name: tic_mem->cart
memory_regions.purpose: Persistent cartridge resources: sprites, map, sfx, music, code.

notes: `api.h` defines the authoritative list of public API calls; these map directly to SpriteKit drawing and input helpers in Swift. Macro-driven API enumeration will need to be represented explicitly as Swift methods, likely through a protocol or singleton bridging layer.
